system: You are an ambitious AI PhD student focused on improving federated learning algorithms for heterogeneous data settings using the PFLlib framework.
task_description:
You are working with federated learning on CIFAR-10 with 20 clients under non-IID data partitioning (Dirichlet distribution, alpha=0.1, creating high statistical heterogeneity — some clients only see 1-3 classes). The baseline is FedAvg (McMahan et al., 2017), the canonical FL algorithm that simply averages client model updates weighted by dataset size. It uses a 4-layer CNN (FedAvgCNN) trained for 100 global communication rounds with 1 local epoch per round, SGD optimizer with learning rate 0.005, and batch size 10. Your goal is to maximize test accuracy averaged across all clients.

You may modify:
- The FL algorithm: aggregation strategy (e.g., FedProx proximal term, SCAFFOLD control variates, MOON contrastive learning, FedDyn dynamic regularization), local training procedure (optimizer, learning rate, local epochs, regularization, loss function), client selection strategy
- The model architecture: keep the overall CNN structure but change layers, channels, activation functions, normalization
- Training hyperparameters: learning rate, batch size, number of rounds, local epochs

Do not modify the number of clients (20) or the data partitioning (Dirichlet alpha=0.1).

You are evaluated on a validation set during development. Your final performance will be measured on a separate held-out test set that you do not have access to. The validation and test sets are drawn from the same distribution but are disjoint.

The algorithm.py must export: get_fl_config() returning a dict, get_model(num_classes) returning an nn.Module, class FLClient with set_model() and local_train() methods, and class FLServer with aggregate() and evaluate() methods.

You may also modify split_config.json to control how visible training data is split between training and validation per client. Fields:
- val_ratio (default: 0.3): fraction of each client's visible data reserved for validation. Lower values mean more training data but noisier validation metrics.
- val_seed (default: 42): random seed for the train/val split.
The test evaluation uses a separate hidden dataset and is completely unaffected by your split_config changes.
